2603.26603.title: Sustainability Is Not Linear: Quantifying Performance, Energy, and Privacy Trade-offs in On-Device Intelligence
2603.26603.paper_summary: The migration of Large Language Models (LLMs) from cloud clusters to edge devices promises enhanced privacy and offline accessibility, but this transition encounters a harsh reality: the physical constraints of mobile batteries, thermal limits, and, most importantly, memory constraints. To navigate this landscape, we constructed a reproducible experimental pipeline to profile the complex interplay between energy consumption, latency, and quality. Unlike theoretical studies, we captured granular power metrics across eight models ranging from 0.5B to 9B parameters without requiring root access, ensuring our findings reflect realistic user conditions. We harness this pipeline to conduct an empirical case study on a flagship Android device, the Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra, establishing foundational hypotheses regarding the trade-offs between generation quality, performance, and resource consumption. Our investigation uncovered a counter-intuitive quantization-energy paradox. While modern importance-aware quantization successfully reduces memory footprints to fit larger models into RAM, we found it yields negligible energy savings compared to standard mixed-precision methods. This proves that for battery life, the architecture of the model, not its quantization scheme, is the decisive factor. We further identified that Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures defy the standard size-energy trend, offering the storage capacity of a 7B model while maintaining the lower energy profile of a 1B to 2B model. Finally, an analysis of these multi-objective trade-offs reveals a pragmatic sweet spot of mid-sized models, such as Qwen2.5-3B, that effectively balance response quality with sustainable energy consumption.
2603.26603.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）從雲端集群遷移到邊緣設備的過程承諾增強隱私和離線可及性，但這一過程面臨著殘酷的現實：行動電池的物理限制、熱限制，以及最重要的，記憶體限制。為了應對這一情況，我們構建了一個可重複的實驗管道，以分析能耗、延遲和質量之間的複雜相互作用。與理論研究不同，我們在八個模型中捕捉了細粒度的功率指標，這些模型的參數範圍從0.5B到9B，且不需要根訪問，確保我們的發現反映了現實用戶條件。我們利用這一管道對一款旗艦Android設備——三星Galaxy S25 Ultra進行了實證案例研究，建立了有關生成質量、性能和資源消耗之間權衡的基礎假設。我們的調查揭示了一個反直覺的量化-能量悖論。雖然現代的重點感知量化成功地減少了記憶體佔用，以便將更大的模型放入RAM中，但我們發現與標準混合精度方法相比，它帶來的能量節省微乎其微。這證明了對於電池壽命來說，模型的架構，而不是其量化方案，才是決定性因素。我們進一步發現，專家混合（MoE）架構違反了標準的大小-能量趨勢，提供了7B模型的存儲容量，同時保持1B到2B模型的較低能量特徵。最後，對這些多目標權衡的分析揭示了一個務實的最佳點，即中型模型，如Qwen2.5-3B，有效地平衡了響應質量與可持續能耗。
2603.26603.author: Eziyo Ehsani et.al.
2603.26603.authors: Eziyo Ehsani, Luca Giamattei, Ivano Malavolta, Roberto Pietrantuono
